quoteFirst: By six oʼclock the storming parties were near the north forts, and a heavy fire was already opened upon them; but they pushed steadily forward until at
quoteLast: the main magazine of the south forts blew up.

quoteFirst: Enemy shells had been falling close to the _Colossus_ and her 5th Division since
quoteLast: , and these ships opened fire at 6.30 P.M.; but the conditions of visibility made it difficult to distinguish the enemy’s battleships.

author: Jellicoe, John Rushworth, 1859-1935

quoteFirst: Looking at the details of the fight from the present time, the most remarkable fact in connection with it seems to be that the _Argus_ endured the raking fire of her antagonist from
quoteLast: until 6.45 P.M., although scarcely able to fire a gun in return.

quoteFirst: During the first fifteen minutes the “Argus” had the advantage of position, and at
quoteLast: raked the “Pelican” at close range, but inflicted no great injury on the enemy’s hull or rigging, and killed at the utmost but one man, wounding only five.
